Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible undernutrition or chronic low glucose. Elevated creatinine may indicate impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for renal impairment and possible dehydration or catabolic state. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, nutritional status, and further evaluation of proteinuria and glycemic control.
